message:
fix(test): adjust readme_groupby_aggregation_matrix to actual agg_list dispatch surface

Follow-on to 1fa74a9. The just-shipped
readme_groupby_aggregation_matrix_compiles_and_runs hit two
discoverability mismatches between the README's "14-name agg() string
dispatch" table and the actual frankenpandas DataFrameGroupBy public
surface as exposed today:

1. agg_list(&[&str]) is the slice-literal-friendly entry point (the
   bare agg() overload set picks the HashMap variant for slice
   literals, which wasn't what the test wanted).
2. agg_list dispatches the 12 value-broadcast-shape aggregations:
   sum / mean / count / min / max / std / var / median / first /
   last / nunique / prod. The remaining 3 names from the README's
   14-row table (sem / skew / kurt|kurtosis) are not exposed via
   the agg_list string dispatch — they only land through the direct
   method calls .sem(), .skew(), .kurt(), .kurtosis().

Adjust the test so:

  - agg_list iterates the 12 strings that actually round-trip.
  - sem(), skew(), kurtosis() are called directly to keep the
    intent of "every README-documented aggregation gets exercised."
    (kurt alias remains agg()-only and is documented inline so the
    next reader doesn't go hunting for it.)

Comment block rewritten to document the agg_list-vs-direct split so
the next maintainer who adds a 15th agg knows which side to extend.

Pure test-correction; no production code changes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
